name: OnTcpConnect
title: OnTcpConnect
syntax: void OnTcpConnect( dword socket, long result);
description: If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when an asynchronous connection operation completes. This means that the connection to the remote station has been established. It is not yet certain at this time that the remote station has accepted the connection with TcpAccept.
If a listen socket of a remote station is blocked, error 10061 (Connection refused) is output in the result parameter.

parameters.name: socket
parameters.description: The socket handle or socket object.

parameters.name: result
parameters.description: The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is 0. Otherwise the value is an error code. Error code 10061 (Connection refused): The listen socket of a server is occupied by another connection that has not yet been accepted with TcpAccept. Because the internal queue for incoming connections is set to 1 in CANoe, only one node can ever queue up there. All other nodes are refused with error 10061.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
